title: Hasselquistia syriaca
intro: Hasselquistia syriaca, also known as Syrian scabious, is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hasselquistia syriaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Hasselquistia syriaca is classified within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This plant is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. It belongs to the genus Hasselquistia, which is a monotypic genus containing only one species. Hasselquistia syriaca is further classified within the subfamily Apioideae, which is a subfamily of plants within the family Apiaceae. Its classification is part of the larger angiosperm plant classification system.
